description: An unspecified but nonempty subset or amount takes 'soli': some people, some time, some water. The word makes no small-or-large judgement. 'wheli' (ANY) leaves every member eligible instead of asserting that a subset is at issue.
articulatory_notes: For 's', hold the tongue blade near the teeth ridge and let air hiss down the centre; round the lips for true-mid 'o'. For 'l', seal the tongue tip briefly at the tooth ridge; keep voicing as air travels along the sides. End on high unrounded 'i'; stress 'so'.
sound_symbolism: The hiss of 's' gathers into rounded 'o', then the tongue touches and releases through 'li'. The brief sequence can suggest taking one loose portion from a larger set.
usage_notes: A bare noun comes straight after 'soli'. In 'soli shemu', some moments, the same form receives its canonical frequency reading, sometimes.
